---
title: "About"
---

I am a recent Master's in Applied Data Science graduate from UNC Chapel Hill with interests in sports analytics, predictive modeling, data visualization, and applied machine learning.

My path into data science was not completely linear. I originally entered college as a chemistry major with the goal of eventually becoming an orthodontist. After my freshman year, I realized that while I respected the field, it was not the area I felt genuinely motivated to build my future around.

That realization led me to change direction and pursue a Bachelor of Science in Economics. Economics felt like a much better fit because it gave me a way to think analytically about real-world behavior, decision-making, incentives, and measurable outcomes. As I moved further into the major, I became increasingly interested in using data to answer practical questions and communicate evidence-based insights.

Through that experience, I discovered UNC Chapel Hill's data science minor, which gave me my first structured exposure to programming, statistical analysis, and applied data work. That minor ultimately became the bridge between my economics background and my decision to continue into UNC's Master of Applied Data Science program.

Since then, my project work has focused heavily on using data science methods to analyze sports performance, forecast outcomes, and communicate insights through reproducible analysis and interactive tools. I am especially interested in projects that combine technical modeling with clear decision-making value, whether through predictive analytics, dashboards, or data-driven storytelling.
